EST. 2026 · A MOVEMENT, NOT A MACHINE DECENTRALIZED POWER · EQUAL LAW · OPEN ELECTIONS VOL. I — NO. 01
For Americans Who Are Done Asking Permission

Two Parties. One Cartel. Time to break it.

If you've ever driven through the night to fight for something Washington told you was hopeless — this was built for you. The duopoly isn't broken. It's working exactly as designed. We're here to redesign it.

Save America From the Fires of Hell
Enlist See the Fight
The Enemy
Isn't Left
or Right.

It's the Duopoly Machine — two brands, one business model, built to recycle power between insiders while the rest of us are sorted into teams and told to hate the other locker room.

And it's Elite Impunity — the quiet arrangement where the wealthy and well-connected live under a different set of laws than you do. We don't fight that with slogans. We fight it with structure: more parties, more competition, equal accountability, and elections that money can't buy.

01
The Action Center

Put Your Weight
Behind It

Featured · Act Now Take 60 seconds

Demand a Single Standard of Justice

No American should walk free for a crime that would put their neighbor in a cell. Tell your representatives to back transparency in sealed-document review, equal sentencing data, and an end to the two-tiered system — so the law means the same thing on every street, in every zip code, for every name.

Send the message →
Transparency

Track the Dark Money

An open, searchable ledger of who is funding whom — across every party. Sunlight on the donors, the PACs, and the pass-throughs that decide your primaries before you ever vote.

Open the ledger →
Defense

Shield the Rogue Independents

When a lawmaker breaks ranks to tell the truth, the machine spends millions to bury them. We organize rapid-response support for principled independents and mavericks under fire.

Defend a candidate →
Sovereignty

Lock Out Bought Elections

American elections belong to American citizens — not the highest bidder, foreign or domestic. We push for hard structural limits on who can purchase influence over your vote.

Read the platform →
Ground Game

Grassroots Organizing Kits

Door-knock scripts, precinct maps, sign-printing files, and a playbook for running a credible independent campaign on sweat equity instead of consultant cash. Free. Downloadable. Yours.

Get the kit →
02
The Political Fight

Back the Brave.
Beat the Bought.

We Back the Brave

The Purple Super PAC exists to fund and defend candidates of any label — or no label at all — who will commit to three things: decentralizing power, restoring one standard of justice, and refusing money that comes with strings to the cartel.

We don't care if there's a D, an R, or nothing next to your name. We care whether you'll vote to break the machine.

We Beat the Bought

And we work to retire the career insiders — the focus-grouped, donor-owned, say-nothing-do-nothing placeholders who've turned public office into a private annuity.

Not with smears. With receipts: their votes, their funders, their record. We put the truth in front of voters and let the truth do the work.

A Parallel Economic Engine

Spend Like You Already Do.
Fund the Fight By Default.

Every household in America already spends thousands of dollars a year on ordinary goods. Right now that money flows straight into the pockets of the corporate state — the same consolidated interests that bankroll the machine we're fighting.

The store flips that. It's a full catalog of everyday products, sourced and fulfilled automatically, where the margin doesn't pad a billionaire's quarter — it funds political warfare against elite impunity and the networks that profit from a captured justice system.

You don't have to change your life. You change where the money lands.

Browse the Store →
1You buy what you already need. Same products, same prices, delivered to your door.
2Fulfillment runs automatically. No warehouse, no overhead skimming the cause.
3The margin becomes ammunition. Proceeds route into accountability campaigns and candidate defense.
4Consumer spend becomes leverage. Ordinary purchases, pointed at extraordinary corruption.

Fund the Resistance to the Machine

Citizen-funded. No corporate cartel money. This is the moment that matters.
